title: podman container ls does not show devcontainer
body: ### Issue Description `podman container ls -a` (nor `podman container ls`) shows running devcontainer. Visual Studio codes "remote explorer" shows the container: ### Steps to reproduce the issue Steps to reproduce the issue 1. Create a file `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` with content ``` { "name": "Testcontainer", "image": "mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:3.13", } ``` 2. Open the folder containing the `.devcontainer` subfolder in visual studio code with the current user 3. Make sure devcontainers are configured and use podman and podman-compse in vs-code 4. Build the container and open it 5. See `Remote Explorer` in vs code and compare to the output of `podman container ls -a` (both executed as the current user) ### Describe the results you received CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES ------------nothing here -------------- ### Describe the results you expected I would expect `podman container ls -a` to show the running devcontainer. ### podman info output ```yaml host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.40.1 cgroupControllers: - cpu - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon_2.1.12-4_amd64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.12, commit: unknown' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 98.68 systemPercent: 0.3 userPercent: 1.02 cpus: 32 databaseBackend: sqlite distribution: codename: plucky distribution: ubuntu version: "25.04" eventLogger: journald freeLocks: 2045 hostname: removed idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 kernel: 6.14.0-23-generic linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 39331147776 memTotal: 64859865088 networkBackend: netavark networkBackendInfo: backend: netavark dns: package: Unknown path: /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/opt/podman/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns version: aardvark-dns 1.15.0 package: Unknown path: /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/opt/podman/libexec/podman/netavark version: netavark 1.15.2 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun_1.20-1syncable1_amd64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.20 commit: 9c9a76ac11994701dd666c4f0b869ceffb599a66 rundir: /run/user/1000/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +WASM:wasmedge +YAJL os: linux pasta: executable: /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/pasta package: Unknown version: | pasta 2025_06_11.0293c6f Copyright Red Hat GNU General Public License, version 2 or later This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it. There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law. remoteSocket: exists: true path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock rootlessNetworkCmd: pasta security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: true seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: false serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/slirp4netns package: Unknown version: |- slirp4netns version 1.3.3 commit: 944fa94090e1fd1312232cbc0e6b43585553d824 libslirp: 4.9.1 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 6 libseccomp: 2.6.0 swapFree: 536866816 swapTotal: 536866816 uptime: 1h 2m 31.00s (Approximately 0.04 days) variant: "" plugins: authorization: null log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan - ipvlan volume: - local registries: search: - docker.io store: configFile: /home/removed/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 0 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home/removed/.local/share/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 1967536746496 graphRootUsed: 39931404288 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Supports shifting: "false" Supports volatile: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 0 runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers transientStore: false volumePath: /home/removed/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 5.5.2 BuildOrigin: brew Built: 1750776105 BuiltTime: Tue Jun 24 16:41:45 2025 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.24.4 Os: linux OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 5.5.2 ``` ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless Rootless ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details other commands like `podman image ls -a` also reveal nothing. ### Additional information Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: can you try with v5.6.0-rc1 that was published recently? Also, any idea if this worked in versions before v5.5.2 or is this your first time trying this?
Any chance it's configured to use the root Podman socket instead of the user's own socket, so the container appears under the root user?

@lsm5 : I just found that no information is available to use such recent versions on ubuntu (kubuntu), and the best I got was via brew (5.5.2), so I would please need further insights into how getting rc1 running on ubuntu.

@agyss its very likely using brew on ubuntu has made some assumptions about your environment.  i'm not sure you'll get a lot of interest on from upstream podman on fixing that route.  So perhaps you could backtrack a bit and we can figure out why podman didn't work for you from apt sources.

on a vm, i do see that `virtiofsd` is available (25.04).  I also just installed podman on 25.04 and it worked flawlessly.

@baude I am using the apt Version now, same issue.

I also confirm nothing worked when the brew Version was uninstalled (means no leftovers etc which could have caused it).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
